name: Texas Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour and sugar. In a heavy sauce pan, combine the butter, shortening, coffee (or water), and cocoa, and heat until boiling.
3. Pour the cocoa mixture over the flour mixture in the bowl, add the buttermilk (or substitute), eggs, baking soda, and vanilla, and mix well using a wooden spoon or high-speed electric mixer.
4. Pour into a well-buttered 17½" x 11 inches jelly roll pan and bake in the 400°F oven for 20 minutes or until the brownies test done in the center.
5. While the brownies are baking, prepare the frosting by combining the cocoa, butter, and milk in a saucepan and heating, stirring to boiling. Then mix in the powdered sugar and vanilla until the mixture is smooth.
6. Pour warm frosting over the brownies as soon as they come out of the oven. Cool, cut, and serve.
Microwave Instructions:
1M. In a large mixing bowl, combine the sugar and flour. In a microwave-safe dish, combine the butter, coffee (or water), and cocoa, and heat on high for 2 minutes until boiling.
2M. Pour the boiling mixture over the flour mixture, stirring to blend. Add the buttermilk (or substitute), eggs, baking soda, and vanilla, mixing well.
3M. Pour the entire mixture into a microwave-safe 9 inches x 11 inches baking dish and nuke at medium-high for 18 minutes, turning the dish every 4 to 5 minutes until done in the center. They may still appear soft on the surface.
4M. Prepare the frosting by combining the butter, cocoa, and milk in a microwave-safe dish and heating on high for 2 minutes. Stir once, add the powdered sugar and vanilla, stirring to blend.
5M. Pour warm frosting over the hot brownies, let cool, cut, and serve.
